title: Process Injection
summary: Process Injection on Windows. Maps to T1055 Process Injection. Required APIs: OpenProcess, WriteProcessMemory.
content:
Behavior: Process Injection
Category: Process Control
Platform: Windows
Risk Level: high
Required APIs: OpenProcess, WriteProcessMemory
Optional APIs: VirtualAllocEx, CreateRemoteThread
Example Sequences: OpenProcess -> VirtualAllocEx -> WriteProcessMemory -> CreateRemoteThread
Semantic Description: The function opens a remote process, writes content into remote memory, and triggers execution in that process.
Malicious Purpose: Often used for stealthy code execution, payload staging, privilege escalation, or evasion.
Evidence Template: Evidence typically includes remote process access plus memory write and remote execution APIs.
ATT&CK Mapping: T1055 Process Injection
Variant Note: Variants may replace CreateRemoteThread with APC or NtCreateThreadEx.
API Details:
- OpenProcess: Open a target process and obtain a handle.
- WriteProcessMemory: Write bytes into another process.
- VirtualAllocEx: Allocate memory inside another process.
- CreateRemoteThread: Start execution in another process by creating a remote thread.

title: Registry Persistence
summary: Registry Persistence on Windows. Maps to T1547 Boot or Logon Autostart Execution. Required APIs: RegOpenKeyEx, RegSetValueEx.
content:
Behavior: Registry Persistence
Category: Persistence
Platform: Windows
Risk Level: high
Required APIs: RegOpenKeyEx, RegSetValueEx
Optional APIs: RegCreateKeyEx
Example Sequences: RegOpenKeyEx -> RegSetValueEx
Semantic Description: The function edits autorun-related registry keys so a payload can execute automatically on boot or user logon.
Malicious Purpose: Used to maintain persistence after system restart.
Evidence Template: Evidence includes access to Run/RunOnce or similar startup-related registry paths.
ATT&CK Mapping: T1547 Boot or Logon Autostart Execution
Variant Note: Common targets include HKCU/HKLM Run and RunOnce paths.
API Details:
- RegOpenKeyEx: Open a registry key.
- RegSetValueEx: Write a registry value.
- RegCreateKeyEx: Create or open a registry key.

title: Download and Execute
summary: Download and Execute on Windows. Maps to T1105 Ingress Tool Transfer. Required APIs: CreateProcess.
content:
Behavior: Download and Execute
Category: Network Communication
Platform: Windows
Risk Level: high
Required APIs: CreateProcess
Optional APIs: InternetOpen, InternetConnect, HttpOpenRequest, HttpSendRequest, URLDownloadToFile
Example Sequences: InternetOpen -> InternetConnect -> HttpOpenRequest -> HttpSendRequest -> URLDownloadToFile -> CreateProcess
Semantic Description: The function establishes a network connection, downloads a payload, and launches it.
Malicious Purpose: Used by droppers and staged malware payload delivery chains.
Evidence Template: Look for a network download primitive followed by process creation on the downloaded file.
ATT&CK Mapping: T1105 Ingress Tool Transfer
Variant Note: Some samples use WinHTTP or raw sockets instead of WinINet.
API Details:
- CreateProcess: Create a new process.
- InternetOpen: Initialize a WinINet session.
- InternetConnect: Connect to a remote server via WinINet.
- HttpOpenRequest: Prepare an HTTP request handle.
- HttpSendRequest: Send an HTTP request.
- URLDownloadToFile: Download a remote resource to a local file.

title: Command and Control Communication
summary: Command and Control Communication on Windows. Maps to T1071 Application Layer Protocol. Required APIs: socket, connect.
content:
Behavior: Command and Control Communication
Category: Network Communication
Platform: Windows
Risk Level: medium
Required APIs: socket, connect
Optional APIs: send, recv
Example Sequences: socket -> connect -> send -> recv
Semantic Description: The function creates a socket, connects to a remote endpoint, and exchanges data.
Malicious Purpose: Used for remote command execution, tasking, and data exfiltration.
Evidence Template: Evidence includes persistent socket communication with bidirectional data exchange.
ATT&CK Mapping: T1071 Application Layer Protocol
Variant Note: Protocol details may require strings, packet formats, or decryption analysis.
API Details:
- socket: Create a socket endpoint.
- connect: Connect a socket to a remote host.
- send: Send data over a socket.
- recv: Receive data from a socket.

title: Input Capture
summary: Input Capture on Windows. Maps to T1056 Input Capture. Required APIs: None.
content:
Behavior: Input Capture
Category: Credential Access
Platform: Windows
Risk Level: high
Required APIs: None
Optional APIs: SetWindowsHookEx, GetAsyncKeyState, GetForegroundWindow, GetWindowText
Example Sequences: SetWindowsHookEx -> GetForegroundWindow -> GetWindowText | GetAsyncKeyState -> GetForegroundWindow -> GetWindowText
Semantic Description: The function monitors keyboard activity and may capture the active window context.
Malicious Purpose: Used to steal credentials and monitor user input.
Evidence Template: Evidence often includes keyboard APIs combined with active window title collection.
ATT&CK Mapping: T1056 Input Capture
Variant Note: Polling-based keyloggers may not install hooks.
API Details:
- SetWindowsHookEx: Install a Windows hook procedure.
- GetAsyncKeyState: Check keyboard state for a key.
- GetForegroundWindow: Get the current foreground window.
- GetWindowText: Read the window title text.

title: System Information Discovery
summary: System Information Discovery on Windows. Maps to T1082 System Information Discovery. Required APIs: None.
content:
Behavior: System Information Discovery
Category: Discovery
Platform: Windows
Risk Level: medium
Required APIs: None
Optional APIs: GetComputerName, GetUserName, GetVersionEx, GetAdaptersInfo, GetLogicalDrives
Example Sequences: GetComputerName -> GetUserName -> GetVersionEx -> GetAdaptersInfo
Semantic Description: The function collects host, user, OS, network, and storage environment details.
Malicious Purpose: Used to fingerprint the environment and guide follow-up behavior.
Evidence Template: Evidence includes clustered host and network enumeration APIs.
ATT&CK Mapping: T1082 System Information Discovery
Variant Note: Some malware also collects locale, domain, or process list details.
API Details:
- GetComputerName: Get the system hostname.
- GetUserName: Get the current user name.
- GetVersionEx: Get operating system version information.
- GetAdaptersInfo: Get network adapter information.
- GetLogicalDrives: Get available drive letters.

title: Bulk File Processing for Encryption
summary: Bulk File Processing for Encryption on Windows. Maps to T1486 Data Encrypted for Impact. Required APIs: CreateFile, ReadFile, WriteFile.
content:
Behavior: Bulk File Processing for Encryption
Category: Impact
Platform: Windows
Risk Level: high
Required APIs: CreateFile, ReadFile, WriteFile
Optional APIs: FindFirstFile, FindNextFile
Example Sequences: FindFirstFile -> FindNextFile -> CreateFile -> ReadFile -> WriteFile
Semantic Description: The function enumerates files and performs repeated read and write operations on them.
Malicious Purpose: Common in ransomware pre-encryption workflows and bulk file tampering.
Evidence Template: Evidence requires directory traversal plus large-scale file access and overwrite patterns.
ATT&CK Mapping: T1486 Data Encrypted for Impact
Variant Note: Confirm real encryption behavior with crypto routines or extension renaming evidence.
API Details:
- CreateFile: Create or open a file, device, or named pipe.
- ReadFile: Read bytes from a file or device.
- WriteFile: Write bytes into a file or device.
- FindFirstFile: Start enumerating files in a directory.
- FindNextFile: Continue enumerating files in a directory.

title: Service-Based Persistence
summary: Service-Based Persistence on Windows. Maps to T1543 Create or Modify System Process. Required APIs: CreateService.
content:
Behavior: Service-Based Persistence
Category: Persistence
Platform: Windows
Risk Level: high
Required APIs: CreateService
Optional APIs: StartService
Example Sequences: CreateService -> StartService
Semantic Description: The function registers a Windows service and may start it immediately.
Malicious Purpose: Used for persistence and privileged background execution.
Evidence Template: Evidence includes SCM operations creating or enabling a service.
ATT&CK Mapping: T1543 Create or Modify System Process
Variant Note: Malware may also pair this with registry edits or dropped service binaries.
API Details:
- CreateService: Create a Windows service entry.
- StartService: Start a Windows service.
